items.title: Show HN: OpenKnowledge – open source AI-first alternative to Obsidian/Notion
items.why_it_matters: Someone shipped an open-source, AI-first knowledge management tool that integrates directly with Claude and Codex, the AI tools you use. This makes it easier to organize your project notes and AI interactions. You could download the MacOS app or use the Web UI+CLI to manage your AI-ingest project notes this week.

items.title: Show HN: A Claude skill that prunes your AI's memory file, one diff at a time
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer shipped a Claude skill designed to clean up the memory file in Claude Code, which you use daily. This directly addresses the problem of AI sessions becoming less effective due to memory bloat. You could try integrating this memory pruning skill into your Claude Code workflow for the AI-ingest project this week to keep your context clean.

items.title: Show HN: DeepSeek Flash inverted the economics of agent products
items.why_it_matters: DeepSeek V4 Flash is an open-source model that offers significant cost reductions (up to 100x) compared to models like Claude Sonnet, while maintaining strong performance. This changes the cost math for building AI-powered projects. You could experiment with integrating DeepSeek Flash into parts of your AI-ingest project where cost is a concern, delegating some coding tasks to it this week to see the cost savings.

items.title: Show HN: Visual multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Someone shipped a visual tool for orchestrating multiple AI agents specifically for Claude Code, which is your primary coding environment. This could make the 'direction part' of your vibe coding much more intuitive and efficient. You could clone this GitHub repo and explore using it to manage different AI agents within your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Show HN: Visual Workspace for Agents Based on Unix
items.why_it_matters: A new visual workspace called Prototyper has been shipped, designed for managing AI agents. It treats everything as a file within a web-based Unix-like environment, which could simplify how you direct and organize your AI agents. You could explore Prototyper as a visual environment to manage and direct your AI agents for the AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: AI-website-cloner-template: Clone any website using AI coding agents
items.why_it_matters: A template has been released that allows you to clone any website using AI coding agents. This is a direct tool for building and shipping, and can help you learn by seeing how AI generates code for existing sites. You could use this template to clone a simple website (perhaps your health reminder site) this week to study the generated code and practice directing the AI.

items.title: What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer shared lessons learned after their AI assistant was subjected to 2,000 hacking attempts. This provides practical insights into making AI-powered applications more robust and secure. You could read this post to understand common vulnerabilities in AI assistants and consider implementing basic security measures in your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Developer to Product Owner: Building Tilores Studio Changed My Mind About LLMs
items.why_it_matters: A developer shared their journey and insights from building Tilores Studio, a project involving LLMs, and how it shifted their perspective towards product ownership. This offers valuable lessons on the process of building and shipping real software with AI. You could read this article to gain perspective on the product development process with LLMs and apply these insights to directing your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Open-source platform for multi-agent workflows
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source platform for managing multi-agent workflows has been released. As you focus on directing AI, tools that help manage multiple agents can streamline your process. You could check out this platform to see if it offers a simpler way to manage multiple AI agents for your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: Claude Skills that turn your AI agent into an expert business operator
items.why_it_matters: A GitHub repository provides 'operator skills' designed to enhance Claude AI agents, potentially making them more capable of handling business-logic tasks. This is a direct tool for improving the agents you direct. You could integrate these 'operator skills' into your Claude Code agent for the AI-ingest project this week to expand its capabilities.

items.title: LLM APIs with built-in chatbot in 1 line of code
items.why_it_matters: A tool called Flama allows you to serve LLM APIs with a built-in chatbot using just one line of code. This simplifies the process of integrating conversational AI into your projects. You could try using Flama to quickly add a chatbot interface to your AI-ingest tool or a new side project this week.

items.title: Show HN: Vibesolve.ai – Turn plain English into Timefold code
items.why_it_matters: Vibesolve.ai is an open-source tool that translates plain English descriptions into Timefold code for optimization problems. While Timefold might be new to you, the core concept of directing AI to generate specific code from natural language is directly relevant to improving your 'direction' skills. You could experiment with Vibesolve.ai to practice clearly articulating coding requirements in plain English this week.

items.title: Show HN: Pith – A local-first desktop LLM wiki without vector DBs or embeddings
items.why_it_matters: Someone shipped Pith, a local-first desktop LLM wiki designed for personal knowledge management without complex setups like vector databases. This offers a simple way to organize information relevant to your projects. You could try Pith as a local knowledge management tool for your AI-ingest project this week, keeping your notes and research organized.

items.title: Show HN: Polygraph – Let AI agents see cross repo and maintain session memory
items.why_it_matters: Polygraph is a new tool that aims to allow AI agents to understand context across different code repositories and maintain session memory. This could make your 'vibe coding' more effective by giving your AI a broader understanding of your project. You could explore Polygraph to see if it integrates with your Claude Code workflow and helps manage context for your AI-ingest project this week.
